# Blocked Tile Damage Feedback

## Context
Life-based blocked tiles take damage from adjacent matches but there's no visual feedback showing the hit. Players can't tell how much damage they dealt.

## Plan

### 1. Add shake animation on damage (`styles/game.css`)
- New `@keyframes blockedShake` - horizontal shake with intensity param
- 3 intensity levels via CSS classes: `.shake-light`, `.shake-medium`, `.shake-heavy`
  - light: ~2px translate (damage < 25% of life)
  - medium: ~4px translate (25-50%)
  - heavy: ~6px translate (>50%)
- Duration ~300ms

### 2. Add floating damage text animation (`styles/game.css`)
- New `@keyframes damageFloat` - move up + fade out (like `formationPowerUpFloat` but positioned on the tile)
- `.damage-text` class - absolute positioned, floats up from tile, red color, bold
- Duration ~800ms

### 3. Animate damage in `unblockAdjacentTiles` (`src/merge-processor.js`)
After applying damage (line 638-652), for each damaged tile that survived (lifeValue > 0):
- Query DOM element by `[data-row][data-col]` or `[data-rect-id]`
- Calculate shake intensity from `damage / (oldLife)` ratio
- Add shake class to element
- Create damage text element (`-{damage}`) and append to tile element
- Update `data-life` attribute to new value
- Remove shake class + damage text after animation completes (~800ms)

### Files to modify
- `styles/game.css` - add shake keyframes + damage-text styles
- `src/merge-processor.js` - add visual feedback in `unblockAdjacentTiles` after damage loop

### Verification
- Play a level with life-based blocked tiles (check config.js for which levels have them)
- Match tiles adjacent to a blocked tile
- Verify: shake plays, damage number floats up in red, life value updates
- Verify: stronger matches produce heavier shakes
- Verify: when tile is destroyed (life <= 0), no shake/damage text plays (existing disappear animation handles it)

content.text:
Both ideas work well together. The shake gives immediate tactile feedback, and the floating damage number lets players see exactly how much they dealt. I kept the scope tight - just 2 files to modify.

One thing to note: the shake intensity scaling by damage-to-life ratio means early small hits feel light, while a big combo that nearly kills the block feels heavy. This gives a natural sense of progress.

# Blocked Tile Damage Feedback

## Context
Life-based blocked tiles take damage from adjacent matches but there's no visual feedback showing the hit. Players can't tell how much damage they dealt.

## Plan

### 1. Add shake animation on damage (`styles/game.css`)
- New `@keyframes blockedShake` - horizontal shake with intensity param via CSS custom property `--shake-intensity`
- 3 intensity levels via CSS classes: `.shake-light` (2px), `.shake-medium` (4px), `.shake-heavy` (6px)
- Duration ~300ms

### 2. Add floating damage text animation (`styles/game.css`)
- New `@keyframes damageFloat` - move up + fade out (similar to `formationPowerUpFloat` at line 1472 but tile-relative)
- `.damage-text` class - absolute positioned within tile, floats up, red color, bold, with text stroke for visibility
- Duration ~800ms, auto-removed

### 3. Animate damage in `unblockAdjacentTiles` (`src/merge-processor.js`)
In the damage application loop (line ~643), for each damaged tile that **survived** (lifeValue > 0):
- Store `oldLife` before subtracting damage
- Query DOM element by `[data-row][data-col]` or `[data-rect-id]`
- Calculate shake intensity: `damage / oldLife` ratio → light/medium/heavy
- Add shake class to element
- Create damage text element (`-{damage}`) positioned on the tile, append to tile element (tile has `position: relative; overflow: hidden` already at line 919-920)
- Update `data-life` attribute immediately to new value
- Clean up: remove shake class + damage text after ~800ms

For tiles that **die** (lifeValue <= 0): no damage feedback needed, existing `disappear` animation handles it.

### Files to modify
- `styles/game.css` - add `@keyframes blockedShake`, `@keyframes damageFloat`, `.shake-*` and `.damage-text` classes
- `src/merge-processor.js` - add damage visual feedback in `unblockAdjacentTiles` damage loop (~line 643)

### Verification
- Play a level with life-based blocked tiles
- Match tiles adjacent to a blocked tile
- Verify: shake plays on hit, damage number floats up in red, life value updates
- Verify: stronger matches produce heavier shakes
- Verify: destroyed tiles (life <= 0) show normal disappear animation, no damage text
- Verify: rectangular blocked tiles with life also get the feedback

# Blocked Tile Damage Feedback

## Context
Life-based blocked tiles take damage from adjacent matches but there's no visual feedback showing the hit. Players can't tell how much damage they dealt.

## Plan

### 1. Add shake animation on damage (`styles/game.css`)
- New `@keyframes blockedShake` - horizontal shake with intensity param via CSS custom property `--shake-intensity`
- 3 intensity levels via CSS classes: `.shake-light` (2px), `.shake-medium` (4px), `.shake-heavy` (6px)
- Duration ~300ms

### 2. Add floating damage text animation (`styles/game.css`)
- New `@keyframes damageFloat` - move up + fade out (similar to `formationPowerUpFloat` at line 1472 but tile-relative)
- `.damage-text` class - absolute positioned within tile, floats up, red color, bold, with text stroke for visibility
- Duration ~800ms, auto-removed

### 3. Animate damage in `unblockAdjacentTiles` (`src/merge-processor.js`)
In the damage application loop (line ~643), for each damaged tile that **survived** (lifeValue > 0):
- Store `oldLife` before subtracting damage
- Query DOM element by `[data-row][data-col]` or `[data-rect-id]`
- Calculate shake intensity: `damage / oldLife` ratio → light/medium/heavy
- Add shake class to element
- Create damage text element (`-{damage}`) positioned on the tile, append to tile element (tile has `position: relative; overflow: hidden` already at line 919-920)
- Update `data-life` attribute immediately to new value
- Clean up: remove shake class + damage text after ~800ms

For tiles that **die** (lifeValue <= 0): no damage feedback needed, existing `disappear` animation handles it.

### Files to modify
- `styles/game.css` - add `@keyframes blockedShake`, `@keyframes damageFloat`, `.shake-*` and `.damage-text` classes
- `src/merge-processor.js` - add damage visual feedback in `unblockAdjacentTiles` damage loop (~line 643)

### Verification
- Play a level with life-based blocked tiles
- Match tiles adjacent to a blocked tile
- Verify: shake plays on hit, damage number floats up in red, life value updates
- Verify: stronger matches produce heavier shakes
- Verify: destroyed tiles (life <= 0) show normal disappear animation, no damage text
- Verify: rectangular blocked tiles with life also get the feedback

Done. Here's what was added:

**CSS (`styles/game.css`):**
- `@keyframes blockedShake` - decaying horizontal shake using `--shake-intensity` CSS var
- 3 shake classes: `.shake-light` (2px), `.shake-medium` (4px), `.shake-heavy` (6px) - selected by damage/life ratio
- `@keyframes damageFloat` - floats up and fades out
- `.damage-text` - red text with dark text-shadow for visibility
- Changed `overflow: hidden` → `overflow: visible` on `.tile-BLOCKED_WITH_LIFE` so damage text can float above

**JS (`src/merge-processor.js`):**
- In the damage loop: stores `oldLife`, then for surviving tiles:
  - Picks shake intensity based on `damage/oldLife` ratio (>50% heavy, >25% medium, else light)
  - Adds shake class to DOM element
  - Creates `-{damage}` floating text appended to the tile
  - Updates `data-life` immediately
  - Cleans up after 800ms
- Works for both single-cell and rectangular blocked tiles
